text: Dietary nitrate supplementation produces a small but statistically significant improvement in exercise performance compared to placebo.
Nitrate supplementation does not produce a significant ergogenic effect in well-trained endurance athletes with VO2max at or above 65 mL/kg/min.
Nitrate supplementation does not produce a statistically significant ergogenic effect in women, though the evidence base is limited.
The ergogenic effect of nitrate supplementation is not modulated by the fraction of inspired oxygen, meaning it works similarly in hypoxic and normoxic conditions.
Nitrate supplementation is most effective as an ergogenic aid in recreationally active men rather than highly trained endurance athletes who have already optimized other training parameters.
The overall effect size of nitrate supplementation on exercise performance is objectively small, suggesting it should not be prioritized over fundamental training and nutrition strategies.
